behaviors.description: Renamed files appear in their own category and are not also counted as a deletion plus an addition.
behaviors.justification: Real failure boundary is the CLI+git interaction: rename detection comes from actual git diff/status semantics, then aggregation must not double-count. Rejected Integration because the behavior explicitly targets repository-derived rename behavior: "Renamed files appear in their own category"; a stubbed parser can miss real git rename output forms and thresholds.
behaviors.test_description: Create a real temporary git repo, commit a file, rename it, run the CLI against the repo, and assert report shows 1 rename with 0 added/0 deleted for that change.

behaviors.description: Diffs that include binary files do not cause the tool to crash; binary files are counted in the output.
behaviors.justification: This depends on real git handling of binary diffs and real CLI subprocess/stdout behavior. Mocking diff text can hide decode/exit-code/path handling failures seen only with actual binary blobs. Rejected Integration due explicit real diff condition: "Diffs that include binary files do not cause the tool to crash".
behaviors.test_description: In a real git repo, add/modify a binary file (e.g., random bytes), run the CLI, assert process exits successfully and binary file contributes to counts.

behaviors.description: Running the tool on a repository that has no commits yet produces an empty report rather than an error.
behaviors.justification: The failure appears at real git repository state + subprocess exit behavior (unborn HEAD/no commits). In-process fakes may not reproduce exact git errors. Rejected Integration because behavior explicitly requires running against real repo state: "repository that has no commits yet".
behaviors.test_description: Initialize a new git repo with no commits, run the CLI, assert zero exit code and an empty structured report instead of error output/exception.

behaviors.description: The counts in the report match the actual change history of the repository being analyzed.
behaviors.justification: This is end-to-end correctness against real git history, including commits and status derivation; the real failure boundary is external git behavior plus tool orchestration. Rejected Integration because the behavior text requires fidelity to "actual change history of the repository being analyzed," which stubs cannot fully guarantee.
behaviors.test_description: Build a real repo history with known added/deleted/renamed/modified events across commits, run the CLI, and compare reported counts to independently computed expected counts from git commands.

behaviors.description: The output is identical whether the changes being analyzed are staged or unstaged.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on real git index vs working-tree semantics and how the CLI queries them; this is OS/filesystem+git subprocess behavior. Rejected Integration because the behavior explicitly compares real git states: "staged or unstaged."
behaviors.test_description: In a real repo, create identical content changes in two scenarios (staged-only and unstaged-only), run CLI for each, and assert structured outputs are identical.
